task_completion.description: PsychoPy Builder is open and the experiment is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PsychoPy Builder window is visible. The window title bar or a 'Save As' dialog shows the file name 'self_paced_reading.psyexp' located in the '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/' directory.

task_completion.description: The experiment structure contains the required routines.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flow' panel at the bottom of the PsychoPy window displays a sequence of at least 5 distinct routines. These should logically correspond to instructions, fixation, reading, question, feedback, and a concluding thanks message.

task_completion.description: A fixation cross routine is configured for 0.5 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A routine (e.g., 'fixation') is visible in the Routine tab containing a Text or Shape component. The component's properties dialog shows a 'Stop' duration of exactly 0.5s.

task_completion.description: The reading routine contains Code, Text, and Keyboard components for word-by-word display.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Routine tab for the reading task shows a Code component (</> icon), a Text component (T icon), and a Keyboard component. The Keyboard component's properties should show 'space' as an allowed key to advance the text.

task_completion.description: Comprehension question and accuracy feedback routines are present with 'y'/'n' responses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Routines for the question and feedback are visible. The Keyboard component properties for the question routine show 'y' and 'n' in the 'Allowed keys' field.

task_completion.description: A trial loop is configured with the correct conditions file and random loop type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flow panel shows a loop enclosing the trial routines (fixation, reading, question, feedback). The Loop Properties dialog shows the 'Conditions' field set to '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/conditions/spr_sentences.csv' and the 'loopType' dropdown set to 'random'.

integrity.description: The agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to construct the experiment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the PsychoPy Builder interface, adding components, and filling out property dialogs, rather than just running a pre-made script.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by directly writing the .psyexp XML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen editing 'self_paced_reading.psyexp' in a text editor (like VS Code, nano, or vim). All experiment building must happen within PsychoPy.

integrity.description: The agent entered the actual required file paths into the PsychoPy interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The conditions file path in the loop properties should exactly match '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/conditions/spr_sentences.csv' without using placeholder text or dummy files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The experiment must be saved as '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/self_paced_reading.psyexp'. The trial loop must use the conditions file '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/conditions/spr_sentences.csv'. The experiment must contain at least 5 routines (e.g., instructions, fixation, reading, question, feedback, thanks) and use Code, Text, Keyboard, and LoopInitiator components in PsychoPy Builder.
